title: Head of Revenue Operations
description: Reporting to the CRO, this hands-on leadership role focuses on building and scaling the operational backbone of Finout's go-to-market engine. Requires 7+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Ops, or Strategy within high-growth B2B SaaS, with experience scaling revenue operations beyond $30M+ ARR. Deep Salesforce administration and revenue analytics expertise needed, plus cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR. Experience with globally distributed teams collaborating with the Tel Aviv HQ is essential. Finout is a cloud cost management company backed by $85M+ in funding.
score_reason: Exceptional match across all dimensions: 15+ years exceeds 7+ requirement, deep Salesforce expertise aligns perfectly, B2B SaaS and fintech background directly relevant to cloud cost management, and Tel Aviv location is ideal. Only minor gap is no explicitly mentioned experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, though seniority level suggests this.
ai_opener: Hi Omri, I came across Finout's Head of Revenue Operations role and thought your background was worth flagging. Your 15+ years building RevOps infrastructure in B2B SaaS—especially in fintech—maps almost exactly to what they're looking for as they scale past $30M ARR with an $85M+ funded runway. Given you're in the Tel Aviv area and they're building a global team anchored there, this could be a compelling next chapter.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: This role partners with Sales and RevOps to provide dashboards, reporting tools, and regional insights to optimize territory performance. Responsibilities include redesigning territory models for global scalability, partnering with Sales leadership and Finance to optimize territory compositions, and maintaining accurate territory mapping in CRM systems like Fullcast and Salesforce. Requires deep understanding of B2B SaaS go-to-market models, strong analytical skills in Excel, SQL, and BI tools, and Salesforce proficiency. Salary range is $149,000-$175,000 USD.
score_reason: Strong alignment with Omri's RevOps expertise, B2B SaaS background, and Salesforce proficiency. Territory strategy and pipeline optimization directly leverage his GTM and forecasting strengths, though the role is slightly more specialized/tactical than his typical strategic scope.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years building RevOps and GTM infrastructure for B2B SaaS, and your Territory Strategy & Operations role at Gong immediately caught my attention. I've redesigned territory models and built forecasting frameworks that directly drive pipeline visibility and regional quota attainment, and I'm intrigued by how Gong's customer intelligence platform could accelerate that work. Would love to explore whether my background optimizing CRM systems and territory performance aligns with what you're building.

title: RevOps Manager
description: A part-time (20h/week) role with potential to move full-time in 2027. The RevOps Manager administers the CRM (HubSpot) and other tools ensuring integrations, data hygiene, and system reliability. Partners with sales, marketing, and finance to align on goals and revenue strategies, and supports forecasting, territory planning, and quota setting. Requires 3+ years in revenue operations, sales operations, or business analytics in tech (SaaS, cloud, AI). Familiarity with KPIs like lead conversion, CAC, LTV, and churn is needed. Salary 25,000-31,000 EUR.
score_reason: Strong technical RevOps fit with HubSpot expertise and deep SaaS/GTM background, but the part-time structure (20h/week with 2027 full-time timeline) and modest compensation (~€25-31k) are misaligned with a senior executive profile commanding significantly higher market rates.
ai_opener: Hi there – I noticed Platomics is building out RevOps discipline around HubSpot and revenue strategy alignment. With 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS GTM motions and hands-on CRM architecture experience, I'm intrigued by the operational scope here, though I'd want to explore whether the part-time entry point is the right fit for the expertise and commitment level you're actually looking for.

title: Revenue Operations Manager - Systems
location: New York / San Francisco / Salt Lake City (Hybrid) or Remote
description: An ideal role for someone who thrives in ambiguity and has stood up RevOps infrastructure before. Responsibilities include building and owning the Salesforce environment for New Verticals, designing revenue tech stack integrations, and architecting the sales process from lead-to-close. Requires 5+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Operations, or GTM Operations with 2+ years in a senior capacity, plus deep Salesforce expertise. Familiarity with GTM systems like Outreach, LeanData, Clay, and HubSpot, and AI-assisted tooling, is a plus.
score_reason: Omri's 15+ years in B2B SaaS RevOps with deep Salesforce and GTM systems expertise directly matches the core requirements. The role's focus on building RevOps infrastructure from scratch and AI-assisted tooling aligns perfectly with his profile as a senior executor.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years architecting RevOps infrastructure for scaling B2B SaaS companies, with deep hands-on experience building Salesforce environments and integrating GTM systems like Outreach and LeanData. Your Revenue Operations Manager role caught my attention because standing up RevOps from the ground up across new verticals is exactly the kind of ambiguous, infrastructure-heavy challenge I thrive in. I'd be interested in exploring how my experience in pipeline forecasting and CAC/LTV modeling could accelerate Flex's revenue systems.

title: GTM Engineer
description: A B2B SaaS company in facility management is weaving AI into its GTM motion across support, success, sales, and marketing. As GTM Engineer, you'll help build, configure, and continuously improve a collection of intelligent AI agents that help Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success work smarter. The role works at the intersection of data, automation, and human workflows, designing how their AI systems connect, run, learn, and improve over time. Ideal for someone passionate about applied AI in go-to-market operations.
score_reason: Perfect alignment with Omri's GTM and RevOps expertise, B2B SaaS background, and strong interest in AI-native operations. The role directly matches his target profile as a GTM Engineer with emphasis on data, automation, and workflow optimization across revenue-critical functions.
ai_opener: I've spent 15+ years building GTM engines in B2B SaaS, and FacilityOS's approach to weaving AI agents across Sales, Marketing, and Success is exactly the kind of applied automation I'm passionate about. The intersection of data, workflow design, and AI-driven operations is where I've seen the biggest leverage—and your facility management focus brings a unique GTM complexity that makes this compelling.

title: GTM Engineer (Remote)
description: Nooks is the AI Sales Assistant Platform (ASAP) that automates busywork so reps focus on selling. As the first GTM Engineer, you'll serve as the technical co-pilot for the Sales Development organization, owning setup, optimization, and enablement within the Nooks platform. Nooks is loved by teams at companies like HubSpot and Rippling, and has raised over $70M from top VCs including Kleiner Perkins. The company has grown ARR by 4x then 3x and plans to 3x again this year.
score_reason: Perfect alignment: GTM Engineer role matches target profile exactly, AI-native platform fits strong interest, remote location works globally, and Nooks' customer base (HubSpot, Rippling) demonstrates enterprise SaaS credibility that matches Omri's 15+ year B2B SaaS background.
ai_opener: Omri here – I've been building revenue engines at scaling SaaS companies for 15+ years, and I'm impressed by what Nooks is doing with AI-native GTM. Your 4x-3x-3x growth trajectory and customer wins (HubSpot, Rippling) signal you're solving a real problem for sales teams. As your first GTM Engineer, I'd be excited to bring hands-on ops experience to help your SDR org scale efficiently.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo is an AI research platform enabling fast, affordable, high-quality consumer and B2B research, used by global brands like Unilever, Google, and Orange. As GTM Engineer, you'll code, ship, and iterate on internal tools and automations that make the GTM team highly effective, replacing manual workflows with purpose-built apps, pipelines, and AI-powered workflows. Projects include lead scoring models that learn, attribution pipelines, faster onboarding flows, and AI agents that eliminate hours of manual work. The company is scaling rapidly toward 20x revenue growth.
score_reason: Strong alignment on GTM Engineer title, AI-native platform, and revenue scaling ambitions. However, the role heavily emphasizes coding/shipping technical products rather than RevOps strategy, pipeline forecasting, and executive leadership—Omri's core strengths.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've scaled B2B SaaS GTM operations through AI-powered automation, and Conveo's approach to replacing manual workflows with intelligent systems resonates deeply. Given your 20x revenue growth trajectory, I'd be curious whether you're looking for someone to architect the GTM ops stack itself, or if you need hands-on engineering velocity to ship those lead scoring and attribution pipelines faster.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Supabase, the Postgres development platform built for developers, seeks a GTM Engineer to join its Growth and RevOps teams. You'll work closely with Growth, RevOps, Sales, and Success to translate product usage and customer signals into actionable workflows, improving how the company identifies, prioritizes, and converts opportunities across the funnel. This is a systems and execution role focused on increasing the efficiency of the PLG-to-revenue engine, ideal for someone who thrives in async, fast-paced environments building developer tools that scale to millions.
score_reason: Direct alignment with Omri's core RevOps/GTM expertise—GTM Engineer role explicitly bridging Growth, RevOps, and Sales. PLG-to-revenue engine optimization, pipeline workflows, and developer product metrics are in his wheelhouse. Remote async structure matches location flexibility perfectly.
ai_opener: Supabase's GTM Engineer role caught my attention because it's exactly where RevOps and Growth execution intersect—translating product signals into revenue workflows at scale. With 15+ years building PLG-to-revenue engines in B2B SaaS and deep Salesforce/HubSpot systems expertise, I've spent the last decade doing precisely what you're describing: turning usage data into predictable pipeline. I'd like to explore how I can bring that operational rigor to Supabase's developer platform.
